subject: Computer Networking
title: Google's 27-Minute Outage (2020)
description: Learn Google's 27-Minute Outage (2020) in one minute.

hook.text: For 27 minutes on December 14, 2020, Gmail, YouTube, and every Google service went completely dark.

buildup.text: Google's authentication system hit a storage quota limit. It couldn't verify any user's identity.

discovery.text: Without auth, every service requiring login rejected all users at once. Global lockout in seconds.

twist.text: The quota was a safety mechanism meant to prevent runaway storage — it caused the outage it was designed to prevent.

climax.text: Engineers freed storage manually. Services recovered, but millions were disrupted during a workday.

punchline.text: A full disk took down the world's largest cloud company.

quiz.question: What triggered Google's December 2020 outage?
options: Their authentication system hit a storage quota limit
A software bug in Chrome browser
An earthquake damaged their primary data center
